channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Colombia's rogue hippos could find refuge in India
item.description: In Colombia, a plan to cull Pablo Escobar's invasive hippos is challenged by an Indian billionaire's offer to relocate dozens of the animals to India's wildlife reserve instead.
item.content:encoded:
In Colombia, a plan to cull Pablo Escobar's invasive hippos is challenged by an Indian billionaire's offer to relocate dozens of the animals to India's wildlife reserve instead.

(Image credit: Fernando Vergara)
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: Contact tracing could be key in halting the spread of hantavirus. Here's how it works
item.description: More than two dozen passengers left the cruise ship before the outbreak was identified. The race is on to connect with them to monitor their health — and the health of those they've interacted with.
item.content:encoded:
More than two dozen passengers left the cruise ship before the outbreak was identified. The race is on to connect with them to monitor their health — and the health of those they've interacted with.

(Image credit: lakshmiprasad S/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: As federal government pulls back, Colorado charts independent course with vaccines
item.description: As state leaders change laws to make vaccines more accessible, a coalition of doctors, public health advocates and everyday Coloradans is trying to start a public conversation about their importance.
item.content:encoded:
As state leaders change laws to make vaccines more accessible, a coalition of doctors, public health advocates and everyday Coloradans is trying to start a public conversation about their importance.

(Image credit: Kevin J. Beaty)
item.dc:creator: John Daley

item.title: Court rejects Virginia redistricting in a blow to Democrats' counter to Trump, GOP
item.description: Virginia voters approved redistricting that could help Democrats pick up four House seats. Democrats said it was to counter the gains that Trump and the GOP have picked up in Republican-led states.
item.content:encoded:
Virginia voters approved redistricting that could help Democrats pick up four House seats. Democrats said it was to counter the gains that Trump and the GOP have picked up in Republican-led states.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Larry Kaplow

item.title: Rooted in nature, 'Silent Friend' will change the way you see the trees
item.description: A new art-house drama tells three stories that span the century — and connect to one tree. Silent Friend will open your eyes to the beauty of the natural world.
item.content:encoded:
A new art-house drama tells three stories that span the century — and connect to one tree. Silent Friend will open your eyes to the beauty of the natural world.

(Image credit: Lena Kettner)
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: U.S. intercepts Iranian attacks on 3 ships. And, what to know about hantavirus
item.description: The U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks yesterday targeting three Navy ships. And, what to know about the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks yesterday targeting three Navy ships. And, what to know about the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.

(Image credit: Amirhossein Khorgooei)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The U.S. fires on 2 Iranian tankers trying to evade its blockade in Gulf of Oman
item.description: The U.S. military said its forces had fired upon and disabled two more Iranian tankers that were trying to breach an American blockade of Iran's ports, in the latest blows to a shaky ceasefire.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military said its forces had fired upon and disabled two more Iranian tankers that were trying to breach an American blockade of Iran's ports, in the latest blows to a shaky ceasefire.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Military drills on the edge: U.S. and allies test capabilities near Asia's flash points
item.description: From drone boats to long-range missiles, the U.S. and its allies tested new military tactics in the Philippines, sharpening deterrence as tensions with China intensify.
item.content:encoded:
From drone boats to long-range missiles, the U.S. and its allies tested new military tactics in the Philippines, sharpening deterrence as tensions with China intensify.

(Image credit: Anthony Kuhn)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Prices are up, but Mother's Day still means brunch
item.description: It's the biggest day for America's restaurants, and one of the biggest for flower sales. Despite anxieties over rising gas prices, people are ready to splurge to celebrate.
item.content:encoded:
It's the biggest day for America's restaurants, and one of the biggest for flower sales. Despite anxieties over rising gas prices, people are ready to splurge to celebrate.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Spain readies for evacuations as a hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands
item.description: Spanish authorities are preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.
item.content:encoded:
Spanish authorities are preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

(Image credit: Misper Apawu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
